item.title: About
item.description: This page is under construction. The story and team behind Plico will be here soon.

item.title: Help
item.description: This page is under construction. Answers to frequently asked questions about Plico will be here soon.

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.description:
Last updated: May 2026.

1. Overview

This policy describes the data processing practices for the Plico website (“Site”) and mobile application for iOS and Android (“Application”). The Data Controller is represented by the Plico developers, reachable at the contact details provided in the “Contact” section. Our goal is to minimise data collection and ensure full transparency.

2. What We Don’t Do

  • No accounts: We do not manage user accounts, registrations, or logins.
  • No profiling: We do not use your data for marketing, advertising, or behavioural profiling purposes.
  • No trackers: We do not embed third-party advertising trackers.
  • No selling: We do not sell or transfer your data to third parties.

3. Mobile Application (iOS/Android)

The Plico application is designed according to the privacy by design principle:

item.title: Terms and Conditions
item.description:
Last updated: May 2026.

The Plico application (hereinafter referred to as “the Application”) is owned by Luigi Gubello and Massimiliano Pippi (hereinafter referred to as “the Owner”). Installing and using the Application implies full acceptance of these Terms and Conditions.

1. License and Intellectual Property

The Application is licensed. The following are prohibited, to the extent permitted by applicable law:

  1. Distributing or reselling the Application to third parties.
  2. Using the Application for unlawful purposes.

For information about third-party open-source licenses used in the Application, see the Third-Party Licenses page.

item.title: Third-Party Licenses
item.description:
Plico is a proprietary application, distributed free of charge. Its source code is not open source, but the Application bundles a number of third-party open-source libraries, listed below together with their license, in compliance with their respective terms.

Android Application

Library Author License
AndroidX Core, AppCompat, Activity, Lifecycle, Room, Biometric Google Apache License 2.0
Jetpack Compose (UI, Material 3, Material Icons Extended) Google Apache License 2.0
Kotlin Coroutines JetBrains Apache License 2.0
Coil Coil Contributors Apache License 2.0
PdfBox-Android Tom Roush (porting of Apache PDFBox) Apache License 2.0
Bouncy Castle (bcprov, bcpkix, bcutil) The Legion of the Bouncy Castle Inc. Bouncy Castle Licence (MIT-style)
CIE ID Android SDK Presidenza del Consiglio dei Ministri / IPZS (Italy) BSD 3-Clause

iOS Application

Library Author License
GRDB.swift Gwendal Roué MIT License
FontAwesome.swift FontAwesome.swift contributors MIT License
OpenSSL-Package Marcin Krzyżanowski (OpenSSL packaging) Apache License 2.0

The full text of each license is available in the official repository of the corresponding library.

item.title: Vulnerability Disclosure Policy
item.description:
Plico is an independent, free project, built from the ground up with a local-first approach focused on protecting the privacy of the people who use it. We develop the iOS and Android apps (cc.plico.app) with as much care as possible, but we know perfect software doesn’t exist.

If you think you’ve found a security bug or vulnerability, write to security@plico.cc.
